perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall protect and safeguard the confidentiality of all such Confidential Information with at least the same degree of care as the Recipient would protect its own Confidential Information, but in no event with less than a commercially reasonable degree of care to protect and safeguard the confidentiality of all such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall protect and safeguard the confidentiality of all such Private Information with at least the same degree of care as the Recipient would protect its own Confidential Information, but in no event with less than a commercially reasonable degree of care to protect and safeguard the confidentiality of all such Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Confidential Information" to "Private Information" in the first instance while maintaining "Confidential Information" later in the sentence creates a contradiction. It's unclear if Recipient needs to protect "Private Information" with the same care it would protect its own "Confidential Information".
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall protect and safeguard the confidentiality of all such Private Information with at least the same degree of care as the Recipient would protect its own Confidential Information, but in no event with less than a commercially reasonable degree of care to protect and safeguard the confidentiality of all such Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information' consistently. The changed text introduces 'Private Information' in one instance while retaining 'Confidential Information' in another within the same sentence. The contradicted text then repeats this conflicting terminology. This creates a misaligned terminology contradiction as 'Private Information' and 'Confidential Information' are used without clear distinction or definition of whether they are the same, leading to ambiguity about the recipient's obligations regarding each type of information. This fits the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, as multiple terms are used interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall not, and s hall cause its Representatives not to, (a) disclose all or any portion of Confidential Information to any third parties (other than to its Representatives who need to know the Confidential Information to assist Recipient in its evaluation of the Purpose ), or (b) use or permit any Confidential Information to be accessed or used for any purpose other than for the evalua tion of the Purpose . Recipient will be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement caused by its Representatives. Notwithstandi ng anything to the contrary contained in this Agreement, Disclosing Party acknowledges and agrees that (a) the Recipient is expressly authorized to disclose the Confidential Information to certain lenders and investors, in furtherance of Recipient’s perfor mance of its obligations with respect to the Purpose and (b) under no circumstances will Recipient have any liability for any disclosure by such lenders or investors of any of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall not, and s hall cause its Representatives not to, (a) disclose all or any portion of Protected Data to any third parties (other than to its Representatives who need to know the Confidential Information to assist Recipient in its evaluation of the Purpose ), or (b) use or permit any Confidential Information to be accessed or used for any purpose other than for the evalua tion of the Purpose . Recipient will be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement caused by its Representatives. Notwithstandi ng anything to the contrary contained in this Agreement, Disclosing Party acknowledges and agrees that (a) the Recipient is expressly authorized to disclose the Confidential Information to certain lenders and investors, in furtherance of Recipient’s perfor mance of its obligations with respect to the Purpose and (b) under no circumstances will Recipient have any liability for any disclosure by such lenders or investors of any of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The use of both "Protected Data" and "Confidential Information" in the same paragraph, without defining "Protected Data," creates confusion. It's not clear which information the clause (a) is refering to, and if the disclosure limitations applies to both.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall not, and s hall cause its Representatives not to, (a) disclose all or any portion of Protected Data to any third parties (other than to its Representatives who need to know the Confidential Information to assist Recipient in its evaluation of the Purpose ), or (b) use or permit any Confidential Information to be accessed or used for any purpose other than for the evalua tion of the Purpose . Recipient will be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement caused by its Representatives. Notwithstandi ng anything to the contrary contained in this Agreement, Disclosing Party acknowledges and agrees that (a) the Recipient is expressly authorized to disclose the Confidential Information to certain lenders and investors, in furtherance of Recipient’s perfor mance of its obligations with respect to the Purpose and (b) under no circumstances will Recipient have any liability for any disclosure by such lenders or investors of any of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Data' in clause (a), while clause (b) still refers to 'Confidential Information'. This misalignment, as highlighted in the perturbation explanation, creates confusion and a potential contradiction because it's unclear whether the disclosure limitations apply to both types of information or just one. This contradicts the goal of clear and consistent terminology within the contract, making enforcement and interpretation ambiguous. The Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, so it further exemplifies that a contradiction exists within the same segment.

perturbation.original_text: Except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, w ithout the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, Recipient shall not disclose to any person or entity (other than its Representatives who are - 2 - involved in its evalua tion of the Purpose ) any information with respect to the occurrence or content of any discussions or negotiations between the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, w ithout the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, Recipient shall not disclose to any person or entity (other than its Representatives who are - 2 - involved in its evalua tion of the Endeavor ) any information with respect to the occurrence or content of any discussions or negotiations between the Parties.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "Purpose" with "Endeavor" without defining "Endeavor" introduces ambiguity. It is not clear whether "Endeavor" refers to the same thing as "Purpose" (defined in the Recitals).
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHE REAS, for the purpose of assisting the Recipient in evaluating the merits of a potential investment in or other financing of the Disclosing Party (the “Purpose ”), Recipient wishes to receive certain information that the Disclosing Party desires to keep con fidential relating to its business plan, financial matters, and products and/or services currently under development ; and WHEREAS, the Disclosing Party desires to disclose such information to the Recipient, subject to the terms and conditions of this Agree ment.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to the "Purpose" which is defined in the contradicted text as 'assisting the Recipient in evaluating the merits of a potential investment'. The changed text replaces "Purpose" with "Endeavor", without defining what "Endeavor" means. This misaligned terminology introduces ambiguity. The Recipient's obligation to not disclose information is now tied to an undefined "Endeavor", creating a contradiction with the earlier defined "Purpose" mentioned in the contradicted text, leading to potential confusion and conflicting interpretations of the non-disclosure obligation, based on the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information in the event and to the extent Recipient reasonably believes any Confidential Information is required to be disclosed by Recipient under the terms of: (i) a valid and effective subpoena; (ii) a statute or regulation binding upon Recipient; (iii) an order issued by a court of competent jurisdiction; or (iv) by a demand or information request from an executive, regulatory or administrative agency or other governmental authority. Recipient shall endeavor to promptly notify the Disclosing Party of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such potential disclosure unless recipient reasonably believes that applicable law prohibits Recipient from infor ming the Disclosing Party of the potential disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient may disclose the Private Data in the event and to the extent Recipient reasonably believes any Confidential Information is required to be disclosed by Recipient under the terms of: (i) a valid and effective subpoena; (ii) a statute or regulation binding upon Recipient; (iii) an order issued by a court of competent jurisdiction; or (iv) by a demand or information request from an executive, regulatory or administrative agency or other governmental authority. Recipient shall endeavor to promptly notify the Disclosing Party of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such potential disclosure unless recipient reasonably believes that applicable law prohibits Recipient from infor ming the Disclosing Party of the potential disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the first instance of "Confidential Information" to "Private Data" while keeping the later instance as "Confidential Information" creates a contradiction. The Recipient has different obligations whether one or the other is to be disclose. There is no way to determine which one.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient may disclose the Private Data in the event and to the extent Recipient reasonably believes any Confidential Information is required to be disclosed by Recipient under the terms of: (i) a valid and effective subpoena; (ii) a statute or regulation binding upon Recipient; (iii) an order issued by a court of competent jurisdiction; or (iv) by a demand or information request from an executive, regulatory or administrative agency or other governmental authority. Recipient shall endeavor to promptly notify the Disclosing Party of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such potential disclosure unless recipient reasonably believes that applicable law prohibits Recipient from infor ming the Disclosing Party of the potential disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces "Confidential Information" with "Private Data" in the Changed Text, while another instance in the same sentence remains "Confidential Information." The Contradicted Text contains the same contradiction in the Changed Text, showing that the alteration introduces a contradiction in the document. The Perturbation Explanation clarifies that the recipient would have different obligations depending on whether "Private Data" or "Confidential Information" must be disclosed, creating ambiguity and a strong, direct contradiction.
